content:
Heya! So I'm finally getting around to this and I wanted to note some things:

1. Everyone with access to the repo has push authority, and I set this up specifically to __not__ use a pull-and-merge system. Push commits to main immediately when you sign Ledgers, and upload your files here for me to download and compile when I can.
2. The reason I don't do pull-and-merge is so that other volunteers' work doesn't get overwritten. For example, if someone had downloaded, worked on, and I compiled Characters.xlsx in ID, all while you were working on it in VN, then if I merge your pull request, the ID work would be overwritten with the old ID column from your file.
3. I presume you did it this way because you either use GitHub Desktop or just plain Git, both of which I'm less familiar with and don't really have good instructions to give other than to just follow the steps in <#1419429157779280035>.
4. You did sign the ledger correctly but didn't push, so I'll put this down as VN Translation since I can't merge your pull request.
5. I did catch a couple examples of a newline with a leading space (`\n `) and one example of a mistyped double newline that was missing its second n (`\n\`) and fixed those. Refer to note (4) in [this message](https://discord.com/channels/1402537664619479100/1416934774525788180/1454937967113273467) for some more info.
6. With all that said, I've compiled the changes present for VN in "Characters (Gateway, The Unknowable, Among the Stars.xlsx" and so that's that! Thanks 😄 

I have some stylistic questions for VN contributors to chew on but this isn't the place for that discussion so I'll drop by in <#1402537664619479107> in a little bit.

Ohhh my that’s a very detail respond 😭 
well with that being said, then from now on can I upload the file here instead since I know the way you are going to do with it? I’m new to Git as well so sometimes I’m not really sure if I’m doing correctly <:horla_haizz:1325112784630841385>
All good! No need to have any prior experience with Git, just follow <#1419429157779280035> as close as you can and everything will go smooth!

And yeah, people say I tend to "overexplain" things, but really I just always want to make sure everything that should be said gets said, in a way that's hopefully hard to misunderstand. Better to have too much information than not enough, imo.
And I like reading helpful information xD
